name: Theo
note: SYNTHETIC — draft for clinical advisor review. De-identified/fictional.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Theo said he had a stomach ache that morning and begged to stay home. At school he went to the nurse during the presentation slot.
entries.parent_response: I let him stay in the nurse's office and emailed the teacher to ask if he could present to her alone later.

entries.situation: Ordering at the ice cream shop
entries.child_behavior_observed: Theo whispered his order to me and asked me to say it to the worker. Wouldn't make eye contact.

entries.situation: Birthday party for a kid in his class
entries.child_behavior_observed: Theo said his stomach hurt and he didn't want to go. Cried in the car. We turned around.
entries.parent_response: We went home. I told him it was ok and we'd try the next one.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Teacher said Theo knows the answers but never raises his hand. Looks down when called on and says 'I don't know' even when he does.
entries.parent_response: This came from the teacher, I wasn't there.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Theo asked the teacher if he could do the project by himself instead of in a group.
entries.parent_response: I emailed asking if he could work solo. Teacher said no.

entries.child_behavior_observed: Theo said he was going to throw up right before reading group and went to the nurse. Seemed fine once I picked him up.

situations: Speaking/performing in front of the class (presentations, reading aloud, raising hand)
Interacting with unfamiliar people (ordering for himself)
Social gatherings and group work (parties, group projects)
parental_responses: Allowing escape/avoidance (staying home, going to nurse, leaving the party)
Emailing teachers to arrange accommodations (present alone, work solo)
safety_behaviors: Feigning illness / somatic complaints to escape (stomach ache, nausea)
Getting a parent to speak for him
Saying 'I don't know' / avoiding eye contact
Requesting solo work to avoid groups
expected_elements.notes: DRAFT answer key — advisor to confirm/correct.
